scenario_title: add_rr and delete_rrset for the same type in one UPDATE
test_rationale: This test creates a single UPDATE that first adds a TXT RR at op.campus.edu. and then deletes the entire TXT rrset at the same owner name, using a helper TXT rrset so the delete is meaningful. Because RFC 2136 does not define whether the server must process these operations strictly in wire order, as a set, or with some conflict rule, different servers may either end with the rrset absent, preserve it, or reject the UPDATE outright. The follow-up QUERY on the touched name exposes the final zone state, while the UPDATE reply code captures the semantic disagreement.

group_1.behavior: Apply UPDATE operations sequentially in wire order, so add_rr followed by delete_rrset leaves no TXT rrset; server may return NOERROR.

group_2.behavior: Treat the conflicting add_rr/delete_rrset as a semantic conflict or process them with a different internal ordering, potentially preserving the added RRset or rejecting the UPDATE.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.3 defines add semantics and §3.4.4 defines rrset deletion semantics, but RFC 2136 does not specify an intra-message evaluation order or conflict-resolution rule when both target the same owner/type in one UPDATE. That leaves both the chosen RCODE and the final rrset state implementation-defined.
